notes: Passed by the canonical rollup: every runnable item passed, and T5 is an honest failed item carrying Dave's explicit 2026-07-16 PostgreSQL-results waiver. T5 was not relabeled pass. No item proves live Google behavior; both Google touchpoints are stubbed at the module seam. Every command below ran independently and its exit code was read directly.

items.title: Every mutating route requires a write-capable role
items.precondition: Run the route tests against the imported Express app with the shared knex database mocked and a signed JWT.
steps: Introspect the router and assert the test table covers every non-GET route.
Call all 21 mutating routes as a viewer.
Call a mutating route as a manager and a read route as a viewer.
items.expected: The coverage table equals the router's real mutating routes; every viewer mutation returns 403; manager mutation and viewer read remain allowed.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/gbp-automation-write-routes.test.ts: 46/46 passed, including router introspection and all 21 viewer-403 cases.
items.notes: The HTTP-method gate is default-secure, so future mutating routes inherit the role check.

items.title: Business-info draft bodies are validated at the route boundary
items.precondition: Run the route suite with an admin member and the draft service spied.
steps: Send malformed nested category, phone, website, and hours payloads.
Send invalid nanos, 24:59, same-day reverse, and zero-length periods.
Send valid overnight, 24:00, all-day, max-nanos, and split-day periods.
items.expected: Malformed payloads return 400 before the controller; valid Google-hours shapes return 201; non-writable keys never reach the staged patch.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/gbp-automation-write-routes.test.ts passed all invalid-body and valid-hours cases.
items.notes: Validation is enforced because this endpoint stages a live external update.

items.title: Queue insertion and compensation failures remain recoverable
items.precondition: Run the business-info service tests with queue.add and compensation model calls controlled at the mock seam.
steps: Fail deploy queue insertion with successful compensation, then retry.
Fail revert queue insertion with successful claim release, then retry.
Make deploy compensation return zero rows and reject.
Make revert compensation return zero rows and reject.
Retry pending deploy/revert states and inspect deterministic BullMQ job IDs.
items.expected: Successful compensation returns draft/releases the claim. Rejected or zero-row compensation returns a recovery-required error and preserves a pending marker. Existing retry/revert actions safely re-enqueue or deduplicate the deterministic job.
items.notes: The service never claims draft/released state unless the compensating update affected one row.

items.title: Build, architecture, lint, and regression gates pass
steps: Run npx tsc --noEmit.
Run npm run check:conventions -- --strict.
Run npx depcruise src.
Run npx eslint src.
Run npm test.
items.expected: Zero type errors, zero clear structural violations, zero dependency/lint errors, and the full backend suite green.
items.evidence: tsc exit 0; strict conventions exit 0 with CLEAR structural violations 0 and runtime files over 800 = 0; depcruise exit 0 with 38 warnings and 0 errors; eslint exit 0 with 2,385 repository-baseline warnings and 0 errors; full suite 551/551 across 63 files. Focused business-info + route suite: 95/95.
items.notes: Commands ran separately; npm run check:all was not used as proof because its semicolon chaining can mask earlier failures.

items.title: Migration applies and rolls back against real PostgreSQL
items.precondition: A real PostgreSQL database with the pre-migration schema.
steps: Run migration up and inspect the business_info columns/check/default.
Run down with no business_info rows.
Run down with rows and confirm refusal without the deletion acknowledgement.
Run down with GBP_BUSINESS_INFO_ROLLBACK_DELETE_ROWS=true.
items.expected: The additive up succeeds; safe down succeeds; data-bearing down refuses by default and succeeds only with explicit deletion acknowledgement.
items.evidence: Not run in this remediation. The migration file is byte-unchanged from reviewed head 6a24ffd7.
items.notes: Failed honestly because live PostgreSQL behavior was not executed. Compile and code review are not runtime database proof.
items.waiver: Dave explicitly waived PostgreSQL results on 2026-07-16 for this PR. This accepts the unrun live migration proof; it does not convert the item to pass or waive deployment-time migration monitoring.

items.title: Multi-table finalization is atomic and failures are not swallowed
items.precondition: Run the business-info tests with the model transaction handing callbacks a sentinel transaction and logger/model calls mocked.
steps: Assert draft and reject status/event pairs share one transaction.
Assert normal provider-success attempt/item/event writes share one transaction.
Assert reconcile provider-success uses the same transaction path.
Fail the publish event after Google success and assert the work item is not marked published outside the transaction.
Assert only the provider receipt remains and the error rethrows for no-second-PATCH reconciliation.
Fail best-effort compensation/revert/notification writes and assert each is logged with identifiers.
items.expected: All atomic write sets share one transaction handle; failed finalization never commits contradictory local state; the provider receipt drives safe retry; every best-effort failure is logged.
items.notes: These prove transaction threading at mocked model seams. Real PostgreSQL rollback is not proven and is covered only by the T5 waiver.

items.title: Explicit attempt and revert states reconcile retries without duplicate Google writes
items.precondition: Run the reconciliation tests with all attempt claim states and both Google touchpoints stubbed.
steps: Drive concurrent_attempt_running, already_succeeded, stale_attempt_running, and claimed.
Run the two-call provider-success/local-failure regression.
Reconcile stale attempts against matching, mismatching, and unreadable live profiles.
Distinguish revert_in_progress from already_reverted.
items.expected: Each state has distinct behavior; Google is patched exactly once across provider-success retry; unreadable provider state never causes a blind write; revert refusal reports the real state.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/gbp-business-info-reconciliation.test.ts passed all claim/reconcile/revert-state cases, including immediate lease takeover on a BullMQ retry; full suite 551/551.
items.notes: No request reaches live Google. SELECT FOR UPDATE behavior is PostgreSQL behavior and is not claimed as proven here.
